title: PDAs in the Enterprise: Analyzing Supplier Viability
author: Isaac Ro; David Wright; Christopher Fletcher
source_file: 2001 PDAs in the Enterprise_ Analyzing Supplier Viability.pdf
abstract: In September 2001, Aberdeen Group evaluated leading PDA vendors using its Market Positioning Pyramid (MPP) framework to assess both product quality and supplier viability. The study argues that brand awareness and market share alone do not determine PDA market leadership. The analysis covers Palm, Microsoft/Pocket PC, Compaq, Handspring, HP, Symbol Technologies, Sony, Toshiba, Casio, Sharp, NEC, Psion, and emerging players.
importance_rationale: Published at the height of PDA market fragmentation, this study provided a rigorous multi-vendor viability framework at a time when enterprises faced real risk from supplier consolidation and platform obsolescence.
relevance_rationale: The MPP framework methodology for evaluating platform/vendor viability in fragmented hardware markets remains applicable; the specific vendor rankings are now purely historical given the iPhone's disruption of the entire category.
prescience_rationale: The study's premise that brand and share don't determine PDA leadership had merit, but the entire PDA market was disrupted and rendered obsolete by the iPhone in 2007 — none of the predicted winners achieved lasting enterprise handheld dominance.
